claims.statement: On this machine and the recorded one-million-cell workloads, DEFLATE level 1 median elapsed time was 322 ms numeric and 406 ms mixed versus 955 ms and 981 ms at level 6; level 1 output was 9.62% and 21.63% larger respectively.

Across the six warmed compression cases, measured benchmark-process peak working set stayed between 6.28125 MB and 6.32812 MB, so the measured compression-level trade-off changed CPU time and output size without material peak-memory growth on these workloads.
All six representative workbooks opened with openpyxl, all runs reported zero active worksheet temporary files after close, and worksheet body buffer peaks stayed below the 262144-byte limit.
